headline: A US export-control directive pulled Claude Fable 5 from all customers as inner-circle channels separately pushed inference past 1,000 tokens per second.
body: The dominant thread is a US export-control directive that forced Anthropic to disable Claude Fable 5 (and Mythos 5) for all customers, with the action reported across the inner circle: Cole Medin benchmarked Fable 5 before withdrawal and questioned its 2x price premium over Opus 4.8 [6], Better Stack cites a purported Anthropic announcement of the immediate shutdown [9], and Cyril Imhof frames the EU access angle [8]. Speed is the other recurring story: Better Stack stress-tested Xiaomi and TileRT's 1-trillion-parameter MoE at 1,000+ tokens per second using FP4 quantization, DFlash speculative decoding, and a persistent GPU kernel [5], and separately benchmarked Google DeepMind's DiffusionGemma, which applies diffusion techniques with bidirectional attention to exceed 1,000 tokens per second on H100s [7]. On tooling, Cole Medin covered Omnigent, an open-source meta-harness that routes tasks across Claude Code, Codex, and Pi with human-in-the-loop governance [0], and IBM Technology argued labs are shifting from raw scale toward smart routing for cost and reliability [3] while covering AI pair programming [1] and MCP-driven design systems [4].
